// description: Golden for the SDDP cut-I/O hot-start round-trip case
— the same converged primal/dual values since loading the converged
cut set into a fresh run and doing a pure sim pass should reproduce
the converged policy exactly — but without the `solver_status` block
because iter=0 (sim-only) runs do NOT emit solver_status.json (no
training iteration, no gap/UB/LB bookkeeping).  The objective check
falls through to `solution.csv` phase=1 obj_value, which pins the

// note: Relative tolerance widened to 2% on 2026-05-15 after CLP
produced a degenerate-policy vertex with the same total
objective (323100) but a different per-phase split: per-phase
costs reshuffled while phase=1's cumulative obj_value still
matched the golden's 323100.  The hot-start-load test exercises
a converged-cut sim pass — the policy IS optimal, but at multi-
vertex degeneracy CLP's simplex pivot order picks among
equivalent vertices and the per-phase decomposition is not
deterministic across CLP minor versions or build flags.  The
TOTAL UB (phase=1 obj_value = 323100) still pins exactly; the
2% rel just absorbs the per-phase reshuffling within a
degenerate optimum.  Phase=1 obj_value is the strict total.

//: Golden for the SDDP cut-I/O hot-start round-trip case under CLP

loading the converged cut set and running a pure sim pass should
reproduce the converged policy exactly — but without the solver_status
block because iter=0 (sim-only) runs do NOT emit solver_status.json.
